14.16: Therefore do not let what you regard as good be spoken of as evil;
14.15: For if because of food your brother is being hurt, you are no longer walking according to love. Do not destroy with your food the one for whom Christ died.
14.17: for the kingdom of God is not eating and drinking, but righteousness and peace and joy in the Holy Spirit.

14.21: It is good not to eat meat or to drink wine or to do anything by which your brother stumbles.
14.13: Therefore let us no longer judge one another, but rather determine this: not to place a stumbling block or an obstacle in a brother's way.

14.23: But the one who doubts is condemned if he eats, because it is not from faith; and whatever is not from faith is sin.

16.7: Greet Andronicus and Junia, my kinsmen and my fellow prisoners, who are outstanding among the apostles, who also were in Christ before me.

16.25: Now to him who is able to establish you according to my gospel and the preaching of Jesus Christ, according to the revelation of the mystery kept secret for long ages—
16.11: Greet Herodion, my kinsman. Greet those of the household of Narcissus who are in the Lord.
16.6: Greet Mary, who has worked hard for you.
16.13: Greet Rufus, chosen in the Lord, and his mother—and mine.
16.21: Timothy my fellow worker greets you, and so do Lucius and Jason and Sosipater, my kinsmen.
16.22: I, Tertius, who wrote this letter, greet you in the Lord.
